phrases.intent_en: Can I pay by card at the cake counter?
phrases.text_local: Kann ich am Kuchenbuffet mit Karte zahlen?
phrases.cultural_note: German cafés increasingly accept card payments, but cash is still common. Direct inquiry avoids assumption.

phrases.intent_en: How do I order the Studentenkuss?
phrases.cultural_note: Ordering at the counter without prior seating is standard in German cafés. Staff expect concise requests.

likely_response.local: An der Theke mit Namen nennen.
likely_response.translation_en: At the counter, state your order.

responses.english: At the counter, state your order.
responses.french: An der Theke mit Namen nennen.

phrases.intent_en: Where do I sit after ordering?
phrases.text_local: Wo setze ich mich nach der Bestellung hin?
phrases.cultural_note: Self-seating is common in casual cafés. Staff may guide if tables are reserved.

likely_response.local: Setzen Sie sich an einen freien Platz.
likely_response.translation_en: Take a seat at an available spot.

responses.english: Take a seat at an available spot.
responses.french: Setzen Sie sich an einen freien Platz.

phrases.intent_en: Is the Schlossblick terrace free now?
phrases.cultural_note: Terrasse availability is typically confirmed by staff or signage. Direct inquiry aligns with German service culture.
